sourceMovie.title: The Interpreter
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Interpreter for the same reason it broke through in 2005: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Sydney Pollack keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Lord of War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Interpreter clicked for you, Lord of War (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Interpreter?
faq.answer: Try Lord of War, Donnie Brasco, 16 Blocks—each shares DNA with The Interpreter while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Interpreter?
faq.answer: The Interpreter (2005) is best described as Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Interpreter worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
